messages.text: Venice soon filled with rumor. One ship from Tripolis was lost, then another from Mexico, then reports from the English coast darkened the Rialto.

messages.name: Salerio
messages.text: Antonio's argosy may have struck the Goodwins. If that is true, his debt to Shylock becomes deadly.

messages.name: Solanio
messages.text: And Shylock runs through the streets crying for his daughter, his ducats, and his jewels in the same breath.
Shylock heard laughter in their pity. Jessica's escape had become public entertainment, and Antonio's loss became his possible revenge.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: You ask why I hate him. He mocked my nation, cooled my friends, heated my enemies, and hindered my bargains.

messages.name: Salerio
messages.text: Surely you will not take his flesh if he cannot pay. What use is it?

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: To feed my revenge. If you prick us, do we not bleed? If you wrong us, shall we not answer?
His words forced Venice to hear a human claim inside a cruel intention. Equal wounds did not make equal mercy.
Tubal arrived with mixed news: more losses for Antonio, and Jessica spending Shylock's money carelessly in Genoa.

messages.name: Tubal
messages.text: One of Antonio's creditors told me a ship was wrecked. Also, your daughter traded a ring for a monkey.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: That ring was Leah's before I married. I would not have given it for a wilderness of monkeys.
Grief and greed tangled until no one could separate them. The more Shylock hurt, the more fiercely he held the bond.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: Let Antonio look to his contract. I will have the penalty. I will not delay.
Meanwhile Bassanio reached Belmont. Portia wished to delay his choice, fearing that one wrong box would send him away forever.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Pause a day or two. If you choose wrong, I lose your company.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: Let me choose. Living with uncertainty is its own torture.
Music began. Bassanio studied the caskets, remembering that appearance cheats and bright promises often purchase shame.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: Gold and silver speak too loudly. Give me the poor lead that threatens rather than promises.
His hand moved toward the plain casket, while in Venice Antonio's losses moved toward the court.
Love waited on one lid; revenge waited on one bond; and both cities held their breath.
The rumor of Antonio's loss traveled faster than any confirmed report. In a trading city, fear itself could become a kind of currency.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: He laughed at my bargains when his ships were proud. Let him learn what a debt means when the sea turns.
Tubal did not comfort him. Each fact arrived with another wound: a ship lost, a jewel spent, a daughter beyond reach.

messages.name: Tubal
messages.text: Other men speak of Antonio with pity. They say no merchant can stand if every venture fails together.
In Belmont, Portia tried to delay because love had made her cautious. Bassanio heard delay as torture because hope had made him brave.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: If I wait, each hour will choose for me. Let my hand risk loss while my heart is honest.
Shylock's revenge grew from injuries that Venice preferred not to count. The play lets him speak those injuries even as it shows his cruelty.

messages.name: Shylock
messages.text: You taught me how to answer harm. Do not complain when the lesson returns.
The rumor of loss therefore became more than business news; it became permission for old anger to step into daylight.
Bassanio did not hear the worst of the rumor until after hope had opened before him. Shakespeare lets joy and danger arrive almost together.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Choose, then. If music cannot calm me, perhaps truth will.
The lead casket waited while Venice tightened around Antonio.
